summary: The diff shows modifications to WorldMapPage.tsx, ATMapPage.tsx, index.html, and supporting files. However, the implementation continues to use Leaflet via CDN (unpkg) rather than installing it as an npm package. REQ-1 and REQ-3 both require removing CDN-based loading and switching to npm-installed Leaflet with direct CSS imports and L.Icon.Default.mergeOptions, but the diff retains CDN tags in index.html and uses 'declare const L: any' (CDN global) in the component files. REQ-2 (AddEntryPage fix) appears addressed per the file summaries. REQ-3 is explicitly violated as index.html still contains Leaflet CDN script and link tags.

missing_requirements.requirement: Install Leaflet as an npm package (leaflet + @types/leaflet), import Leaflet CSS directly in the component files, fix default marker icon broken asset paths by explicitly setting L.Icon.Default.mergeOptions with correct icon URLs pointing to leaflet/dist/images assets, and ensure map container div has an explicit pixel height.
missing_requirements.reason: WorldMapPage.tsx and ATMapPage.tsx still use 'declare const L: any' (CDN global), not an npm import. There is no 'import leaflet/dist/leaflet.css' or 'import L from leaflet' in the component files. L.Icon.Default.mergeOptions is not called. The components rely on the CDN global rather than the npm package.

missing_requirements.requirement: Remove all Leaflet CDN script and link tags from index.html so Leaflet is loaded exclusively via the npm package import.
missing_requirements.reason: index.html still contains both a Leaflet CDN link rel=stylesheet and a script tag loading Leaflet from unpkg. The requirement is explicitly not met.
